admission_type: evaluation/CV (uncapped number of students; admission by evaluation of the admission requirements / qualification assessment by the Academic Committee)
admission_test_type: Master-level qualification/CV evaluation by the Academic Committee; no entrance test. Per the A.Y. 2026/2027 course-specific requirements table, Quantum Engineering does NOT require a Syllabus, does NOT require a GRE score, and has no online interview requirement (no entry in the 'Other requirements' column).
applicant_route: Italian qualification (PoliTo or other Italian university bachelor) — handled via the Engineering-area Italian-qualification calls
non-EU applicants legally residing in Italy with a valid residence permit
non-EU applicants residing abroad and requiring a visa (the relevant route for Turkish applicants with a non-Italian qualification)
admission_call_round: 1st call (A.Y. 2026/27, non-Italian qualification): applications 19 December 2025 – 13 February 2026, 2:00 p.m. (Italian time); results ~15 April 2026
2nd call: applications 18 March 2026 – 20 April 2026, 2:00 p.m. (Italian time) (closing postponed due to technical issues); results ~22 June 2026

A Specific Call for International Tracks (24 April – 18 May 2026, 23:59) exists but does NOT apply to Quantum Engineering — it is only for Physics of Complex Systems International Track and Micro and Nanotechnologies for Integrated Systems (MNIS). Quantum Engineering is admitted through the standard 1st/2nd calls.
application_platform: Apply@polito (didattica.polito.it Apply portal — pkg_apply registration) for the application; UNIVERSITALY portal for non-EU visa pre-enrolment after the Confirmation of Eligibility is issued.

academic_requirements.degree: A Bachelor's degree (EQF Level 6) or an equivalent academic qualification issued by an accredited foreign university, giving access to second-cycle/master's study.
academic_requirements.schooling_and_credits: At least 15 years of global schooling (minimum 3 years at university level) with at least 180 ECTS credits or equivalent.
academic_requirements.curricular_requirements: Applicants must meet the programme-specific curricular requirements, assessed by the Academic Committee. Recommended background to apply to Quantum Engineering (A.Y. 2026/2027 course-specific requirements table): 'Any field of Engineering, Physics, Mathematics'. The programme page additionally lists suitable backgrounds as Electronic Engineering, Physics Engineering, Computer Engineering, Telecommunications Engineering and Mathematics for Engineering, and also Physics, Computer Science and Mathematics.
academic_requirements.programme_specific: Syllabus is NOT required for Quantum Engineering (course-specific requirements table). No GRE score is required. No online interview is required. The programme is strongly multidisciplinary, training engineers to apply quantum technologies in computing, communication and sensing.
academic_requirements.evaluation: Admission is by evaluation of the admission requirements (uncapped number of students); meeting the requirements does not guarantee admission until the Academic Committee assessment is completed.

country_specific_eligibility: Applicants with a non-Italian qualification (e.g. a Turkish bachelor's degree) must, for enrolment, provide a CIMEA Statement of Comparability (Attestato di Comparabilità) OR a Dichiarazione di Valore (Declaration of Value) issued by the Italian Embassy/Consulate in the country where the qualification was awarded. The foreign degree must be a valid official qualification giving access to second-cycle studies and meet the 15-years-schooling / 180-ECTS / minimum-3-years-university rule. [uncertain]
minimum_background_or_credits: Minimum 180 ECTS credits and a bachelor's degree (EQF Level 6) with at least 3 years at university level. Recommended disciplinary background for Quantum Engineering: any field of Engineering, Physics, or Mathematics. No published numeric GPA threshold or fixed minimum CFU per subject area for this programme; curricular adequacy is judged by the Academic Committee. [uncertain]

language_requirements.english_level: English level B2 or higher (CEFR). The A.Y. 2026/2027 course-specific requirements table states a minimum of IELTS 5.5 for Quantum Engineering.
language_requirements.italian_requirement: No Italian certificate is required: Quantum Engineering is a fully English-taught programme (Italian B2 is required only for Italian-taught or partially-Italian/English programmes).
language_requirements.accepted_certificates: Accepted English certificates include IELTS, TOEFL, TOEIC and Cambridge; the full list with minimum scores is in the PoliTo Language Centre (CLA) certificate chart.
language_requirements.exemptions: Exemptions from the English certificate typically include qualifications fully taught in English (e.g. IB Diploma in English, high-school qualifications from the USA, Canada, UK, Ireland, Australia, New Zealand and from English-medium schools in specified countries), per the general PoliTo language rules. [uncertain]
language_requirements.minimum_score_reference: Minimum IELTS 5.5 (equivalent to B2) per the A.Y. 2026/2027 course-specific requirements table.

english_certificate_codes: For TOEFL/TOEIC, applicants should indicate Politecnico di Torino institution code C285 when requesting that scores be sent.
language_submission_timing: The English language certificate is a compulsory application document and must be uploaded in Apply@polito at the time of application (within the call deadline); it is required to evaluate eligibility for the English-taught programme.

application_deadline_eu.note: EU applicants and non-EU applicants residing in Italy with a non-Italian qualification apply through Apply@polito within the same Master's calls; the dedicated non-Italian-qualification page governs both EU and non-EU non-Italian-qualification applicants.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: 1st-semester online enrolment closes 5 October 2026; mandatory enrolment documents to be uploaded by 5 October 2026.
application_deadline_eu.no_visa_route: EU applicants and non-EU applicants already residing in Italy do not use the UNIVERSITALY visa pre-enrolment route.

application_deadline_non_eu.first_call: 1st call application closing: 13 February 2026, 2:00 p.m. (Italian time) (call opened 19 December 2025). Results published ~15 April 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.second_call: 2nd call application closing: 20 April 2026, 2:00 p.m. (Italian time) (call opened 18 March 2026; closing postponed due to technical issues). Results published ~22 June 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.applies_to_turkish_applicants: This non-EU / non-Italian-qualification route is the relevant one for Turkish applicants residing abroad and requiring a visa. Quantum Engineering uses the standard 1st/2nd calls (NOT the special International-Tracks call).
application_deadline_non_eu.study_visa_document_deadline: Study Visa Type D document upload deadline: 30 November 2026.

universitaly_preenrolment_deadline: Non-EU applicants residing abroad and requiring a visa must complete the visa pre-enrolment on the UNIVERSITALY portal (https://www.universitaly.it/) after receiving the Confirmation of Eligibility: 1st-call candidates by 30 May 2026; 2nd-call candidates by 15 July 2026. This is separate from and required in addition to the Apply@polito application; admission to PoliTo does not automatically guarantee the visa.
application_fee: €50.00 non-refundable application fee, paid online by credit card when submitting the Apply@polito application.
required_documents: Passport (Latin alphabet; non-Latin scripts require official translation)
Bachelor's degree (EQF Level 6) OR an official university document stating the provisional graduation date
Official Transcript of Records (with grading system: minimum/maximum, GPA, and credits)
English language certificate (B2 / minimum IELTS 5.5), unless exempt
Syllabus / course descriptions of bachelor's studies — NOT required for Quantum Engineering (per the course-specific requirements table)
For enrolment (not at application): CIMEA Statement of Comparability OR Dichiarazione di Valore (Declaration of Value) from the Italian Embassy
Optional but recommended: recommendation letters, motivation letter, work-experience certificates
document_translation_authentication: Documents in Italian, English, French or Spanish are accepted without translation; documents in other languages require an official translation into English or Italian merged into a single file. Italian translations must be certified by the Italian Embassy in the issuing country or sworn before an Italian Court translator (asseverazione). For enrolment, foreign-qualification recognition is via a CIMEA Statement of Comparability or a Dichiarazione di Valore (Declaration of Value). Documents must be on letterheaded paper, stamped and signed by the issuing institution (digitally signed documents accepted if verifiable via QR code/verification link).

entry_exam_or_test.type: No entrance/admission test. Admission is by evaluation of qualifications/CV by the Academic Committee.
entry_exam_or_test.gre: GRE is NOT required for Quantum Engineering (no entry in the 'Other requirements' column of the course-specific requirements table, unlike Computer Engineering, Cybersecurity, Data Science, Mechatronic and Computer Engineering which list 'GRE optional but highly valued').
entry_exam_or_test.interview: No online interview is required for Quantum Engineering (unlike PoliTo masters such as Electrical Engineering or Systemic Design which require an online interview).
entry_exam_or_test.mode: Application and evaluation are conducted entirely online via Apply@polito.

test_score_or_threshold: English requirement threshold: B2 / minimum IELTS 5.5. There is no published numeric ranking threshold or GPA cut-off for Quantum Engineering; admission is an uncapped evaluation of whether the applicant meets the curricular and language requirements as judged by the Academic Committee. [uncertain]

source_quotes.quote: QUANTUM ENGINEERING — English — IELTS 5.5 — Any field of Engineering, Phyisics, Mathematics — Syllabus: Not Required
Degree class: LM-29: ELECTRONIC ENGINEERING. Admission: Uncapped number of students, evaluation of the admission requirements. Teaching language: English. Campus: Main Campus.
The Master's degree programme in Quantum Engineering is entirely taught in English. ... Department of Electronics and Telecommunications, Collegio di Ingegneria Elettronica.
All courses across both years are flagged English; the programme is a single curriculum of 120 ECTS over 2 years with no Italian-language options.

1st Call: Closing 13 February 2026, 2:00 p.m. (Italian time); Results 15 April 2026; Visa pre-enrolment deadline 30 May 2026. 2nd Call: Closing 20 April 2026, 2:00 p.m. (Italian time); Results 22 June 2026; Visa pre-enrolment deadline 15 July 2026.

Bachelor's degree (level 6 EQF) or an equivalent academic qualification ... at least 180 ECTS credits or equivalent and a minimum requirement of 15 years of global schooling (minimum 3 years at University level).

English-taught programmes: English level B2 or higher. Accepted English certificates: IELTS, TOEFL, TOEIC, Cambridge. For TOEFL/TOEIC indicate the code C285 to share the results with Politecnico di Torino.

Compulsory documents: Passport, Curriculum Vitae, Bachelor's degree, Official Transcript of Records, English/Italian Language Certificate, Dichiarazione di valore or Statement of Comparability issued by CIMEA.
Pay the non-refundable application fee (€50) online by credit card.
Visa pre-enrolment (UNIVERSITALY): 1st Call deadline May 30, 2026; 2nd Call deadline July 15, 2026. Study visa document submission deadline 30 November 2026.

PHYSICS OF COMPLEX SYSTEMS - International Track** - SEPARATE CALL FOR ADMISSION; MICRO AND NANOTECHNOLOGIES FOR INTEGRATED SYSTEMS** - SEPARATE CALL FOR ADMISSION. (Quantum Engineering is NOT marked as a separate call.)

uncertainty_notes: BROAD English rule satisfied and exceeded: Quantum Engineering is catalogued as 'English', the official Academic Regulations state it is 'entirely taught in English', and the curriculum page shows all courses across both years (120 ECTS, 2 years) flagged English with no Italian-language options. Therefore is_fully_english_programme = true and has_complete_english_track = true; english_track_name is null because the whole programme is English (no separate track).

Degree class confirmed as LM-29 (Electronic Engineering / Ingegneria elettronica) from the official programme page and search results. This resolves and CORRECTS the item-context placeholder 'LM-44 / LM-17' and its note to 'Confirm exact degree class on programme page.' — PoliTo classifies Quantum Engineering under LM-29, NOT LM-44/LM-17. The programme is run by the Department of Electronics and Telecommunications (Collegio di Ingegneria Elettronica).

2026/2027 is the governing cycle (confirmed on the non-Italian-qualification Master's page and on the programme page). The Quantum Engineering Academic Regulations PDF available at the time of checking was labelled A.Y. 2025/2026; the 2026/27 Student Guide may still be under construction, but the course-specific requirements table and admission pages are explicitly A.Y. 2026/2027.

Country-specific eligibility for a Turkish bachelor's degree (CIMEA Statement of Comparability vs Dichiarazione di Valore path) is inferred from the general non-Italian-qualification rules; no Turkey-specific clause was published for this programme.

No numeric GPA cut-off or fixed minimum CFU-per-subject is published for Quantum Engineering; curricular adequacy is assessed by the Academic Committee, so minimum_background_or_credits and test_score_or_threshold are partly qualitative.

IELTS 5.5 is stated in the course-specific requirements table; the general non-Italian-qualification page phrases the English requirement as 'B2 or higher'. These are consistent (IELTS 5.5 maps to B2). Exact accepted-certificate minimum scores for other tests (TOEFL, TOEIC, Cambridge) are in the external CLA certificate chart, which was not parsed item-by-item; the English-exemption list is inferred from PoliTo's general rules. [uncertain]

Tuition fee amount not stated numerically: PoliTo sets the non-EU comprehensive yearly fee by proportioning the maximum fee to the applicant's country income/GDP, with a stated minimum; use the official fees page / simulator for an exact estimate.

Quantum Engineering does NOT require a Syllabus, a GRE score, or an online interview (per the course-specific requirements table — its 'Other requirements' cell is blank). This distinguishes it from neighbouring Electronics-area masters such as Computer Engineering and Data Science (GRE optional but highly valued) and Electrical Engineering (online interview).

The official_call_url is the central 'applicants-with-a-non-italian-qualification' Master's page, which functions as the governing call for non-EU / non-Italian-qualification applicants; PoliTo does not publish a single per-programme PDF call for Quantum Engineering.

The Special Call / Specific Call for International Tracks (24 Apr – 18 May 2026, 23:59) applies ONLY to Physics of Complex Systems International Track and Micro/Nanotechnologies for Integrated Systems (MNIS); Quantum Engineering uses the standard 1st/2nd calls (special_call_flag = false).
